pages.full_text:
Half-term at __Dinas Camp__ near __Harlech__. May
Simon 39, Eve 35, Thomas 9, William 7,
Peter 6 (just) John 21 months, Charles 6 weeks –

Lovely campsite in the hills.
Rubber tyre for the boys to swing on.
East wind tent. .
We forgot the can opener.
The boys played ball with Liverpool family,
including man in a wheelchair.
Eve backed into a wall.
Simon carried John in his push chair
all the way up to the castle at Harlech from
the carpark. The van worked beautifully. Hot
showers, comfortable bed.
    We visited the river for swimming,
Harlech castle, sandy beach (including sandy
icecreams) and had a picnic at the Roman
steps.
    On walks, Simon carried John on his
[continued right page] Eve carried Charles in [?Phyllida]'s
headings: Half-term at Dinas Camp near Harlech. May
pages.notes: Blue decorative asterisks/stars appear above the words 'near Harlech' in the heading — possibly a rating. 'East wind tent. .' as written with period and then space and another period. Bottom of page cut off; text continues on right page. 'Phyllida's' may refer to a baby carrier belonging to someone named Phyllida, or a brand name — reading uncertain. Year 1989 estimated from position (page 4 of 350, volume starts 1989); confidence low.

pages.full_text:
carrier, Thomas carried the food
(beef sandwiches, apple, choc bar, water[illegible])
and William sometimes carried the
spare clothes.
Almost Contenious work, glorious sunshine
+ a fabulous holiday – even time to
read + write letters

    Simon was very nervous about Eve's
driving, and only stopped concentrating for
her when Peter bit into a "blood" sweet in
the back seat....

                                    June 10th
Malvern old boys' day. A very useful base
here. Joined Robert Upex. Mrs. Hill at
No 1.
pages.notes: Page continues from left page (page 2). 'Almost Contenious work' transcribed verbatim (likely intended 'Continuous'). Two horizontal underlines appear in the text: one beneath 'read + write letters' and one beneath 'the back seat....'. 'June 10th' date appears right-aligned, possibly a separate entry or sub-heading. 'Malvern old boys' day' may refer to an Old Boys event at Malvern College; 'Robert Upex' and 'Mrs. Hill' are personal names. 'No 1' likely refers to a house number address. Year 1989 estimated from volume position; confidence low as June 10th entry could be a different year.
